text: Clone your voice for every demo. Record fifteen seconds of natural speech, and Argo turns it into narration that sounds like you. All local, all private — no data leaves your machine.
The workflow is three steps. Record a reference clip. Preview the cloned output. Then run the full pipeline. Each step runs entirely on your machine.
Four reasons to clone. Total privacy — everything stays local. Fast iteration with the preview script. High fidelity from Qwen3 tee tee ess. And zero rewrite — same manifests you already use.

Step one: record your reference. Run the recording script, read a phoneme-rich sentence, and the tool captures fifteen seconds of clean audio. It applies noise reduction and normalization automatically.

Step two: preview. The voice clone preview script sends your voiceover manifest to the M.L.X. audio server. It generates each clip individually so you can listen, tweak, and iterate before committing.
Step three: configure. Add M.L.X. audio as your tee tee ess engine, point it at your reference clip, and you're done. One config change, and every argo pipeline run uses your cloned voice.
Three models to choose from. Spark for speed. Qwen3 for the best clone quality — it captures your tone, pacing, and vocal character. And C.S.M. for conversational style.
And now, the new argo preview command. After your first pipeline run, open a browser-based viewer to edit voiceover text, adjust overlays, and regenerate tee tee ess per scene — without re-recording.
From a fifteen second voice clip to a polished demo video. Your voice, your brand, powered by Argo.
